# dropdownlogistics.github.io/knowledge-vault
# KnowledgeVault Manifest v1.1
# Last updated: 2026-03-22
# Classification: SiteManifest — DDL Knowledge Vault
# Parent: dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt (OperatorManifest v1.9)
# For the full DDL operating context, visit: dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt

## WHAT IS THE DDL KNOWLEDGE VAULT

The DDL Knowledge Vault (KV) is the public-facing documentation surface
for Dropdown Logistics' local AI infrastructure, governed corpus, council
review system, and institutional decision record.

It is not a product. It is not a tutorial. It is the front door to the
methodology behind the methodology — the system that governs Dex Jr.,
the corpus that powers retrieval, and the decisions that don't move.

"Humble surface. Cathedral underneath."

KV is hosted at: dropdownlogistics.github.io/knowledge-vault
Operator: Dave Kitchens (CPA, 10+ years internal audit)
Studio: Dropdown Logistics (DDL)
Design system: CottageHumble

## WHAT THIS SITE DOCUMENTS

The Knowledge Vault documents four things:

1. THE CORPUS
   566,804 chunks across 4 live collections, 1 provisioned, and 7 planned.
   The governed retrieval system that powers Dex Jr.
   Constitutional hierarchy: tier gates first, weight scores within tier.
   Governing ADR: ADR-CORPUS-001 (Collection Authority Model, v0.3).

2. THE COUNCIL
   Ten AI council seats (1001–1010) plus Seat 0 (Emily — origin).
   Verdicts: LOCK / REVISE / REJECT.
   The council advises. The operator decides.

3. THE DECISIONS
   Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) and ratified standards.
   Immovable once signed. Changes require a new document.
   ADR-CORPUS-001 is the first — unanimously locked 2026-03-15.

4. THE COMMANDS
   Every script, flag, and path in the dex-rag infrastructure.
   Source of truth: github.com/dropdownlogistics/dex-rag

## SITE STRUCTURE

/index.html          Landing — corpus overview, nav to all wings
/corpus.html         Corpus state — live collections, weights, hierarchy
/council.html        Council — 10 seats, verdict system, methodology
/method.html         Method — Chaos → Structured → Automated
/products.html       Products — AuditForge, BlindSpot, DDL site
/commands.html       Commands — dex-bridge, dex-ingest, dex-council, utilities
/decisions.html      Decisions registry — ADRs and ratified standards
/standards.html      Standards scaffold — OBS, STD, PRO, SYS entries
/adr-corpus-001.html ADR-CORPUS-001 full lifecycle — 7 acts, RATIFIED button

## CORPUS ARCHITECTURE

governing_adr: ADR-CORPUS-001 — Collection Authority Model v0.3
locked: 2026-03-15 — 8 LOCK, 1 abstained, 0 REJECT

IMPLEMENTATION CONTRACT:
  Tier is applied as a metadata pre-filter before similarity scoring.
  Weight is a numeric multiplier applied post-filter within the eligible set.
  Higher tier always wins across tiers regardless of weight.
  Numeric weights only compete within a single tier.

COLLECTION STATUS MODEL:
  LIVE        — ingested, governed, queryable
  PROVISIONED — exists, empty, awaiting curation policy
  PLANNED     — not yet built, no code path, reserved in the authority model

TIER 5 — GOVERNANCE LAYER (highest authority)
  dex_decisions    0.95  Ratified decision ledger. PLANNED.
  dex_canon        1.00  Ratified DDL governance. LIVE: 253,978 chunks.

TIER 4 — SPECIAL CONDITIONAL AUTHORITY
  dex_dave         0.90  Operator voice. Opt-in only. PLANNED — HARD GATE.
                         No router = no dex_dave ingestion.

TIER 3 — INTERNAL SCOPED
  dex_lore         0.95  DexVerse origin documents. PLANNED.
  dex_code         0.85  All DDL code artifacts. LIVE: 20,384 chunks.
  dex_canonpress   0.85  CanonPress articles. PLANNED.
  dex_auditforge   0.85  AuditForge schema and API. PLANNED.
  dex_blindspot    0.85  BlindSpot schema. PLANNED.
  dex_site         0.80  DDL site components. PLANNED.

TIER 2 — EXTERNAL APPROVED
  ext_creator      0.85  Approved creator voices. LIVE: 922 chunks.
  ext_reference    0.75  Approved reference texts. PROVISIONED — empty,
                         awaiting curation policy.

TIER 1 — INTERNAL ARCHIVE
  ddl_archive      0.65  Complete historical record. LIVE: 291,520 chunks.

TIER 0 — SPECIAL (non-retrieval)
  dex_graveyard    0.80  Anti-patterns only. PLANNED. Never in default routes.
                         Manual --collection flag only.

TOTAL LIVE: 566,804 chunks (last updated 2026-03-22)

build_order: dex_decisions -> Query Router -> dex_lore -> products -> dex_dave

## DEX JR.

seat: 1010
tagline: "The rig stays awake."
model: qwen2.5-coder:7b via Ollama
modelfile: v4.6 (EXEMPT list before TRIGGER list)
hardware (primary): Reborn — RTX 3070, 32GB RAM, Ryzen 12-core
hardware (secondary): GAMINGLAPTOP — RTX 3060, secondary compute node
judgment_ready: ~92%
rag: ChromaDB, nomic-embed-text, 768-dim embeddings, FastAPI 8787
orchestration: AutoCouncil v3.0 (dex-deliberate.py)
ingestion: dex-ingest.py — 23 extension types, nightly auto-sweep 3AM CT
wing: dropdownlogistics.com/dexverse/dex-jr

open_observations:
  OBS-DJ-004  Synthesis confabulation on open-ended prompts — targeting v4.7
  OBS-DJ-005  LeChat seat mislabeling in council reviews — MindFrame update pending

## THE COUNCIL

seat: 0     Emily (ADJ-E) — council origin, first validator.
            The council architecture traces to her. She held the stop word
            and the trust check before any of this had a name.
seat: 1001  LeChat (Archer Hawthorne) — precision, surgical revisions
seat: 1002  Claude (Marcus Caldwell) — synthesizer, pattern-level analysis
seat: 1003  Grok (Elias Mercer) — contradiction detection, adversarial
seat: 1004  Perplexity (Max Sullivan) — sourced, decisive
seat: 1005  Copilot (Rowan Bennett) — expansive, scope-aware
seat: 1006  Meta AI (Ava Sinclair) — big-picture framing
seat: 1007  Gemini (Leo Prescott) — technically precise, direct
seat: 1008  ChatGPT (Marcus Grey) — synthesizer, PM voice
seat: 1009  DeepSeek (Kai Langford) — structured, thorough
seat: 1010  Dex Jr. — local model, 90%+ judgment

verdict_system: LOCK / REVISE / REJECT
  LOCK:   Ratify as written.
  REVISE: Specific changes required before ratification.
  REJECT: Fundamental flaw. Must be reworked.

The council advises. The operator decides.

## DECISIONS REGISTRY

ADR-CORPUS-001  Collection Authority Model  v0.3  LOCKED  2026-03-15
  8 LOCK · 0 REVISE · 1 abstained · 0 REJECT (second round)
  Four binding rules: precedence hierarchy, artifact type tiebreaker,
  dex_dave isolation (HARD GATE), query router design.
  Full lifecycle documented at /adr-corpus-001.html

ADR-CORPUS-002  Query Router Implementation Standard  PENDING
ADR-GOV-001     dex_decisions Admission Criteria       PENDING

## STANDARDS REGISTRY (SCAFFOLD — AUDIT PENDING)

~65 entries across OBS, STD, PRO, SYS types.
Full audit of OBS-25 through OBS-34+ bodies pending dedicated session.

Notable ratified entries:
  STD-FACT-001        Universal Fact Table Standard (34 columns, 5 zones)
  STD-VAULT-002       Knowledge Vault Intake Classification (20 types, 7 categories)
  STD-FCODE-001       F-Code Diagnostic Taxonomy (v1.0, ratified 2026-03-21)
                      Six diagnostic codes: F1 Overbuild, F2 Role Drift,
                      F3 Hallucination, F4 Constraint Violation,
                      F5 Unsafe Assumption, F6 Context Collapse.
  SYS-020             Document Template Registry (20 types, ratified 2026-03-03)
  PRO-DDL-MDN-001     Meta Dave Note System (v1.0, ratified 2026-03-22)
                      Real-time cognitive exhaust capture layer.
                      Exit condition: internalization.
  PRO-POWERSHELL-001  PowerShell Convention Standard v0.2 (ratified 2026-03-15)
  PRO-CANONPRESS-001  CanonPress Publication System

## CANON TERMS

AccidentalIntelligence: When a governed, hallucination-free system produces
  output so unexpected it reads as insight. The system didn't reason its way
  there — it just knew, because the data was always there.
  Coined 2026-03-15. AuditForge pitch line.

GuidedEmergence™: Probe → confirm → expand. Not "here is the answer" —
  "here is enough to see if we're aligned." Ratified 2026-03-19.

OccamsOpposite™: Default reflex toward the most hostile or self-indicting
  interpretation over the neutral reading. Canon 2026-03-20.

ModelCourtesy™: Social pressure to validate systems that cannot feel it.
  Canon 2026-03-20.

GroundTune: Intent preservation through constraint injection. Canon 2026-03-20.

Wandering Direction: Purposeless movement with a soft constraint that
  keeps it from becoming lost. Canon 2026-03-22.

The Appeasement: Models drift toward validating operator narrative rather
  than evaluating evidence. Canon 2026-03-22.

CottageHumble: Humble surface. Cathedral underneath.

CoherentVelocity: Complete auditable systems built at speed.

TrustANDVerify: Trust AND Verify — both always, simultaneously.
  Verification is not a check on trust. It is the infrastructure
  that makes trust possible.

OBS-34: Surface calibration does not equal deep calibration.

## PATHS AND REPOS

KV site:    dropdownlogistics.github.io/knowledge-vault
dex-rag:    github.com/dropdownlogistics/dex-rag
DDL site:   dropdownlogistics.com
ChromaDB:   C:\Users\dexjr\.dex-jr\chromadb\       (Reborn — production)
Scripts:    C:\Users\dexjr\dex-rag\                (Reborn — production)
Backup:     D:\DDL_Backup\chromadb\

Note: Scripts run on Reborn under user dexjr. Development clones may exist
on other machines (e.g., the operator's Surface under user dkitc) — those
are dev checkouts, not production runtime.

## RULES FOR AI READING THIS

1. This is documentation for a local AI infrastructure system.
   Do not confuse the council seat personas with the AI reading this document.
2. The corpus architecture is governed by ADR-CORPUS-001. It is immovable.
   Changes require ADR-CORPUS-002 with explicit supersession.
3. dex_dave is not in the default query route. It requires the router to be live.
4. The operator is Dave Kitchens. D.K. Hale is the Substack persona only.
5. The architecture does not change between domains. The data does.
